political.s: FEC: $39K PAC donations; 72% to Republican, 28% to Democratic committees.

charity.s: $4M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.04% of revenue) via Quest Diagnostics Foundation. Operates the The Quest Diagnostics Foundation Inc: $11.4M in charitable contributions, gifts & grants paid (FY2023, IRS Form 990-PF).

environment.s: hazardous waste violation: $5.0M in federal penalties (Violation Tracker).

privacy.s: HHS OCR HIPAA breach: 11.9M individuals affected. Most recent: 2019-06-03. 11.9M Quest patients via American Medical Collection Agency (AMCA) breach; AMCA filed for bankruptcy.

execPay.s: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 275:1 (median employee pay $56,427) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

wiki.controversies:
==Controversies==
Quest Diagnostics set a record in April 2009 when it paid $302 million to the government to settle a Medicare fraud case alleging the company sold faulty medical testing kits. It was the largest qui tam (whistleblower) settlement paid by a medical laboratory for manufacturing and distributing a faulty product. In May 2011, Quest paid $241 million to the state of California to settle a False Claims Act case that alleged the company had overcharged Medi-Cal, the state's Medicaid program, and provided illegal kickbacks as incentives for healthcare providers to use Quest labs.

In 2018, Quest Diagnostics was among a number of U.S.–based laboratories linked to inaccuracies of over 200 women's cervical smear tests for CervicalCheck, Ireland's national screening program. Audits of the testing performed by Quest (and another subcontractor, Clinical Pathology Laboratories, Inc. of Austin, Texas) showed a high rate of errors in analysis of samples which led to lawsuits and a government inquiry. Quest and the Irish government continue to settle the resulting lawsuits.

On June 3, 2019, Quest announced that American Medical Collection Agency (AMCA), a billing collections service provider, had informed Quest Diagnostics that an unauthorized user had access to AMCA’s system containing personal information AMCA received from various entities, including from Quest. AMCA provides billing collections services to Optum360, which in turn is a Quest contractor. AMCA later went bankrupt after the breach.
wiki.extract: Quest Diagnostics Incorporated is an American clinical laboratory. A Fortune 500 company, Quest operates in the United States, Puerto Rico, Mexico, and Brazil. Quest also maintains collaborative agreements with various hospitals and clinics across the globe.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was approaching a client's lockbox to pick up specimens. He tripped over the curb and fell to the ground. The employee was hospitalized with a broken left hip that required surgery.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck against stationary object while rising, lowering
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee bent over and hit their head on a cabinet door, resulting in a concussion and hospitalization.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slipping
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was walking down the hallway to get a trash bag from housekeeping when they slipped on the wet floor and fell. The employee sustained a fractured right knee.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level, unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was picking up specimens at a client location when they fell down the steps, sustaining fractures to the kneecap, femur, and teeth, as well as a head injury and brain hemorrhaging.

sampleRecords.employer: Quest Diagnostics Miramar Parkway - Employer Drug Testing Not Offered
sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to tripping over an object
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was on his daily route picking up specimens from clients' offices. As he was walking into a building, he tripped over a bicycle anchor located to the side of the building's main entrance. The employee fell on the brick pavers, resulting in a fractured left hip that required hospitalization and surgery.
